articles.title: Quote of the Day by Roman Emperor Trajan: “If I fulfil my duties, use [the sword] for me; if I fail... – Inspiring lessons on leadership, accountability, justice, integrity and why with great power comes great responsibility by the optimus princeps ("the best ruler") known for his enduring philosophy of ethical leadership and governance
articles.description: Quote of the Day by Trajan: Trajan’s quote, “If I fulfil my duties, use [the sword] for me; if I fail, against me,” explores the importance of accountability, integrity, and responsible leadership. The quote suggests that true authority should always be accompanied by a willingness to accept responsibility for one's actions. It reminds us that leaders earn trust not through power alone but through fulfilling their duties honestly and accepting the consequences if they fail to uphold the standards they expect from others.

articles.title: Israel and Lebanon Sign Framework Agreement in Washington: Key Details
articles.description: Israel and Lebanon signed a framework agreement in Washington on Friday, mediated by the Trump administration, laying out steps toward eventually ending Israel's occupation of southern Lebanon. The deal includes two pilot withdrawal projects near the Litani River.

articles.title: In the midst of raging riots in Belfast, this Indian woman did what Indians do best
articles.description: As anti-immigrant riots spread through Northern Ireland, the writer turned fear into what she had learned growing up in India – cooking and delivering meals to people across Belfast – and discovering how kindness could travel across a frightened city

articles.description: Congress calls for a Supreme Court probe into alleged embezzlement of Ram Temple donations, demanding disbandment of the temple trust.

articles.title: What is Umuganda? This country's monthly ritual helped it rebuild after genocide
articles.description: Although Umuganda is the subject, the star of the film is Paul Kagame, a one-time Tutsi general who became Rwanda’s president in 2000.

articles.title: Coimbatore teachers seek exemption from census duty, cite impact on students’ academics and sports
articles.description: Coimbatore's postgraduate and physical education teachers are appealing to the district administration for exemption from national census duties. They argue that census work will severely disrupt crucial Class XII exam preparation, syllabus completion, and vital school sports activities.

articles.title: Young Indian Parliament-2026 begins, over 150 students take part
articles.description: The two-day Young Indian Parliament (YIP 2026), organised by Young Indians (Yi), Mysuru, an initiative of the Confederation of Indian Industry (CII), one of its flagship national initiatives aimed at nurturing responsible, informed, and future-ready leaders through experiential parliamentary learning was inaugurated by SP Mallikarjun Baladandi here on Friday in the presence of Young Indians members, school principals, teachers, jury members, and student delegates.

articles.title: Majlis Bachao Tehreek raises concern over SIR enumeration, criticises AIMIM
articles.description: Majlis Bachao Tehreek criticizes AIMIM over SIR enumeration issues, urging adherence to Election Commission guidelines for voter enrolment.

articles.title: 'Outsourcing deepens sense of hopelessness': Even promise of government job fading for Kashmir’s conflict generation
articles.description: Debate over outsourcing nearly 23,000 government posts has intensified concerns among educated youth about shrinking public sector jobs. The Omar Abdullah government says the move meets manpower needs under existing rules.

articles.title: 'Lord Ram will drive them out': Kejriwal jabs BJP amid Ayodhya temple donation ‘theft’ row
articles.description: Arvind Kejriwal appealed for a social boycott of those accused of their involvement in the alleged embezzlement of donations to the Ram Mandir in Ayodhya.

articles.title: Amid Ram Mandir row, UP deputy CM brings in Babri Masjid, madrasas: ‘Nobody asking...’
articles.description: UP deputy chief minister Brajesh Pathak questioned why no one was asking about the status of funds collected for the Babri Masjid in Ayodhya.

articles.title: Bangladesh Jamaat Steps Up Attack On PM. A Look At Its Influence On Parliament
articles.description: The parliamentary election of 2026 became the greatest electoral success of Jamaat throughout its history.

articles.title: Transfer of Katchatheevu to Sri Lanka another 'historic dark chapter' of Congress rule: BJP
articles.description: The BJP has sharply criticised the Congress, labeling the 1974 transfer of Katchatheevu island to Sri Lanka as a "historic dark chapter" that undermined India's national interests. Party spokesperson Sudhanshu Trivedi highlighted the ongoing difficulties faced by Tamil Nadu fishermen due to this decision. He accused the Congress of a pattern of "surrender," citing historical instances like the partition and territorial concessions.

articles.title: How Gilgit-Baltistan's Sectarian Divide Was Built, Not Born
articles.description: Gilgit-Baltistan (GB) was a syncretic region where Shias, Sunnis, and Ismailis coexisted, traded, and intermarried. However, over the last few decades, this social cohesion has been systematically eroded by political elites.

articles.title: More than 100 women players sign up for ICC's protection programme against online trolling
articles.description: Over 100 women cricketers are now protected from online abuse through the ICC Player Protection Programme, powered by AI. During the T20 World Cup, nearly 60,000 harmful comments were removed, with repeat offenders facing restrictions. Players like Radha Yadav and Amy Jones highlight the program's importance in combating social media toxicity and safeguarding athlete well-being, allowing them to engage with fans more confidently.

articles.title: CCTVs removed as Rabri Devi prepares to vacate official residence
articles.description: CCTV cameras were removed from former Bihar CM Rabri Devi's official residence amid an eviction process. She has been given a final ultimatum to vacate the Patna bungalow by June 29, failing which legal action will be initiated.

articles.title: ‘Protest hallmark of democracy’: Court drops case against farmer leader over hunger strike
articles.description: The Madras High Court was hearing a petition filed by an office-bearer of the Tamil Nadu Farmers Protection Association, seeking cancellation of criminal proceedings pending before a trial court in Tiruppur district.

articles.description: Champat Rai and Anil Mishra have resigned after the Ayodhya Ram Mandir donation row intensified and the SIT flagged lapses. The move comes as arrests, political demands and scrutiny over temple offerings deepen concern among devotees.

articles.description: Amid chants and a birthday song, Dipke urged Pradhan to resign, suggesting he would provide a resignation letter as a gift.

articles.description: The Delhi High Court has directed Meesho to remove listings of innerwear products found prima facie similar to the registered Jockey trademark.

articles.title: Dettol ad portraying women as 'dirty' over past relationships spark outrage, legal concerns in China. Video
articles.description: According to reports, the four-minute commercial was produced to promote the company's laundry sanitiser and was released in late May. Although it initially attracted little attention, excerpts later circulated widely on Chinese social media after being shared by bloggers, prompting a wave of criticism.

articles.description: Subramanian Swamy claimed Prime Minister Narendra Modi had \"no role\" in the Ram Mandir legal battle and urged him to \"withdraw from the construction of Ram Mandir\" amid the ongoing Ayodhya temple donation embezzlement controversy.

articles.title: 'Lovely Country Of Iran...': Trump Flips Script, Drops Bombshell On Israel & Americans | WATCH
articles.description: Speaking after the Iran conflict, President Trump said the U.S. is expanding agricultural exports worldwide, highlighting a potential new market in Iran and claiming China has committed to purchasing at least $17 billion in U.S. farm products, primarily soybeans. Trump touted a sharp reduction in the agricultural trade deficit, vowed continued support for ethanol producers, and promised billions in farm relief, portraying his trade agenda as a major boost for American agriculture.#Trump #DonaldTrump #AmericanFarmers #ChinaDeal #Iran #USFarmers #Soybeans #FarmExports #TradeWar #USPolitics #IranWar #China #Agriculture #BreakingNews #TrumpSpeech
